title: Chondrophylla prostrata
taxonomy: Chondrophylla prostrata is classified as a species within the genus Chondrophylla, which is a group of flowering plants belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family consists of over 600 genera and approximately 13,000 species. Within the family Rubiaceae, the genus Chondrophylla contains a limited number of species, all of which are native to specific regions. The classification of Chondrophylla prostrata provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and ecological context.
parts: Chondrophylla prostrata is a species of plant that has been used in traditional medicine. The leaves and roots of the Chondrophylla prostrata plant are believed to have medicinal properties, although the specific uses and benefits are not well-documented. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever and digestive problems.
